pr.title: ft/ftKirby: decompile Kirby special N and Hi moves
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled several Kirby special-move functions in ftKb_SpecialN.c and ftKb_SpecialNNs.c: Final Cutter ground/air enter routines, Inhale swallowed-item/fighter proximity checks, and Kirby-with-Pikachu-copy Skull Bash collision/wall-bounce handling. The diff also added MWCC dont_inline pragmas for matching control and an extern collision box declaration. Automated reporting recorded two new exact matches and several near-complete improvements.

key_files.role: Implemented Kirby Final Cutter enter functions and Inhale capture proximity helpers; added ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby include and dont_inline pragmas.
Implemented Kirby/Pikachu-copy SpecialN collision transitions and wall-bounce helper; declared ftKb_Init_803CB758 collision box.

what_changed: Replaced stubs for ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter and ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter. Both reset cmd_vars and mv.kb.specialhi fields, change to ftKb_MS_SpecialHi1 or ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi1, call ftAnim_8006EBA4, spawn effect 0x494, set x2219_b0, and install efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks.

Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 for swallowed item checks. It builds an inhaled-mouth position from cur_pos plus ftKb_DatAttrs specialn_x_offset_inhaled and specialn_y_offset_inhaled, compares it_802F23AC distance against specialn_inhale_velocity squared, calls it_802F2810, then branches to air or ground follow-up states.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F5EA8 for swallowed fighter checks with the same mouth-position and threshold pattern, using ftCo_800BD19C and ftCo_800BD620 on fp->victim_gobj.
Added dont_inline pragmas around ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 and around the capture animation callsite region, matching the PR note that MWCC otherwise inlined 800F5DE8 into callers.

Implemented ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Coll, changing Kirby/Pikachu-copy aerial Skull Bash startup into ground motion states when the collision helper indicates a transition, preserving cur_anim_frame and restoring callbacks death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, deal_dmg_cb, and x21F8.

Implemented ftKb_PrSpecialN_Coll, detecting wall collision with ft_800824A0 and ftKb_Init_803CB758, changing to ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNEndR, restoring callbacks, copying self_vel.x into gr_vel, and zeroing self_vel.y/z.

Implemented fn_80105978 as a bounce/reversal helper: sets model Y rotation from M_PI_2 * facing_dir and negates horizontal velocity, ground velocity, ground acceleration, multiple mv.pr.specialn velocity/direction fields, facing_dir, and x34 vector components.
Only minor formatting changed in fn_800F98F4 for Luigi fire item/effect spawn calls.
smart_moves: Used explicit dont_inline control instead of forcing source structure alone to carry function boundaries through MWCC optimization.

Kept item and fighter Inhale proximity logic parallel while preserving their type-sensitive differences: item path uses target_item_gobj, it_802F23AC, it_802F2810, and f64 dist; fighter path uses victim_gobj, ftCo_800BD19C, ftCo_800BD620, and f32 dist.
Reloaded or re-scoped Fighter pointers after motion-state changes in several routines, matching a common decomp tactic for register lifetime and callback assignment patterns.
Preserved current animation frame and callback hooks when changing Skull Bash motion states, avoiding behavioral loss during ground/air or wall-cancel transitions.
Represented the wall-bounce helper as full state reversal, not just a velocity flip, which matches the PR body description that direction variables and model facing must all be updated.
decomp_lessons: For fighter move enter functions, reset both generic command variables and move-specific union fields before calling Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Effect-spawning move startup may need hitlag integration: spawn the effect, set the effect-active flag, and assign pre_hitlag_cb/post_hitlag_cb to pause/resume effect libraries.

Kirby Inhale swallow checks are driven by data attributes for mouth offsets and inhale velocity; matching source should compute the offset from cur_pos and facing_dir instead of hard-coding coordinates.
When a helper should remain visible as a separate function, use MWCC pragmas such as #pragma dont_inline on/off around the relevant function or callsite region.

Copied-special collision states often restore fighter callbacks immediately after Fighter_ChangeMotionState; future work should look for repeated death2_cb, take_dmg_cb, deal_dmg_cb, and x21F8 restoration blocks.
Wall-bounce behavior may require negating every related motion-state field, not only self_vel or facing_dir.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter functions use the ftKb_ prefix and live under src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby.
Ground/air variants are named with Special versus SpecialAir, as in ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter and ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter.
Move callback suffixes identify hook type: _Enter for action entry, _Anim for animation callbacks, and _Coll for collision callbacks.
Unresolved or role-uncertain helpers retain address-style names such as ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 and fn_80105978.
Motion state constants follow ftKb_MS_* naming, for example ftKb_MS_SpecialHi1, ftKb_MS_SpecialAirHi1, ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNStartL, ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNLoop, and ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNEndR.
Data-attribute fields for Kirby Inhale use descriptive specialn_* names such as specialn_x_offset_inhaled, specialn_y_offset_inhaled, and specialn_inhale_velocity.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma dont_inline on/off was explicitly added to prevent MWCC from inlining ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 into capture animation callers.

Parallel functions were not forced into identical source spelling: ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter resets cmd_vars with separate descending assignments, while ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter uses a chained assignment, likely reflecting different codegen needs visible in the diff.

The item Inhale helper uses f64 dist plus casts to (int*) fp->target_item_gobj and (float*) &pos, while the fighter helper uses f32 dist and a normal Vec3 pointer, preserving call ABI/source-shape differences.
Inner scoped blocks with a fresh Fighter* fp2 appear after Fighter_ChangeMotionState in the collision routines, a source-shaping pattern useful for matching callback assignment codegen.
Exact constants were kept in source, including effect ID 0x494, flags 0x0C4C5092U, float comparisons with 1.0F, and M_PI_2 for rotation.
An extern ftCollisionBox ftKb_Init_803CB758 was declared so ft_800824A0 can take the collision box address directly instead of using an unnamed literal.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided slice.
Automated encounter report for GALE01 showed matched code 59.41%, +0.01%, +368 bytes.
Automated report listed two new 100.00% matches: ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter and ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter.
Automated report listed improvements but not exact matches for ftKb_SpecialN_800F5EA8, ftKb_PrSpecialN_Coll, fn_80105978, ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Coll, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8, and .sdata2.

The PR body says fuzzy_match_percent was 100.0 for all functions, but the automated report excerpt shows several items still below 100% exact matched-code reporting; treat exact-match status as needing final build verification.
